methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

path: accountManager.getPlayer(_lockRecipient) - verify main account and registration
Update lockedToken storage (remainder, quantity, lastLockTime, unlockTime)
data_flows.trust_changes: User-provided _onBehalfOf is trusted as lock recipient without recipient consent; _lockDuration derived from recipient's stored settings or fallback to lockdrop.minLockDuration
validation_points: Lock duration within lockdrop range (only if lockdrop active)
exposure_risks: lockedToken state is fully overwritten for unlockTime and lastLockTime on each lock call, including lock-on-behalf
manipulation_risks: CRITICAL: lockOnBehalf allows anyone to reset victim's unlockTime by locking tiny/zero amount, extending their lock indefinitely
Fee-on-transfer tokens: contract records _quantity but receives less, creating accounting surplus for the user on unlock
Zero quantity lock on ERC20 still updates lastLockTime and unlockTime, enabling zero-cost griefing via lockOnBehalf
Remainder from previous lockdrop is zeroed if new lock occurs outside lockdrop period

path: unlock entry point with notPaused and nonReentrant
Transfer ETH via .transfer() or ERC20 via .transfer()
data_flows.trust_changes: No trust change; msg.sender verified as token owner
exposure_risks: ETH sent via .transfer() with 2300 gas stipend may fail for smart contract wallets
ERC20 .transfer() return value not checked (though OZ ERC20 reverts on failure)
manipulation_risks: If fee-on-transfer tokens were locked, user can withdraw recorded _quantity which exceeds actual contract balance eventually leading to last-user-can't-withdraw
Remainder is not returned on unlock, locked forever if user fully unlocks

path: For each token with quantity > 0: check new unlockTime not before old unlockTime
manipulation_risks: HIGH: The reduction check compares (block.timestamp + _duration) vs old unlockTime, but the ACTUAL new unlockTime is (lastLockTime + _duration). When significant time has passed since lastLockTime, a small _duration passes the check but sets unlockTime to a past timestamp, enabling early unlock.
No minimum duration check - user can set duration to 0
Unbounded iteration over configuredTokenContracts array could cause DOS

path: approveUSDPrice: other operators approve, incrementing approvalsCount
_execUSDPriceUpdate: sets usdPrice on all listed token contracts
transformations: Single proposedPrice applied to ALL contracts in the proposal
data_flows.trust_changes: 3-of-5 multisig threshold elevates individual feed proposals to executed price updates
validation_points: Role check for all three operations
exposure_risks: USD price directly affects getLockedWeightedValue which determines reward distribution
manipulation_risks: HIGH: A disapprover can ALSO approve the same proposal since disapproveUSDPrice only sets disapprovals[msg.sender] but approveUSDPrice only checks approvals[msg.sender]. This means a single operator can inflate both counters.
MEDIUM: Same price applied to all contracts in proposal - cannot set different prices for different tokens in one proposal, and a malicious proposer could include unrelated tokens
LOW: With APPROVE_THRESHOLD=1, the proposer's auto-approval should trigger execution but _execUSDPriceUpdate is not called in proposeUSDPrice, requiring 2 approvals minimum regardless of threshold

path: For each active token with quantity > 0: compute weighted value
transformations: Decimal normalization: quantity scaled up by deltaDecimal
sinks: Return value used by external callers for reward calculation
data_flows.trust_changes: Relies on admin-set decimals and multisig-set usdPrice
exposure_risks: If configuredToken.decimals > 18, the subtraction underflows (Solidity 0.8.25 reverts), DOSing the function for all users
Manipulated usdPrice directly inflates/deflates weighted values
manipulation_risks: Admin misconfiguration of decimals can brick the weighted value calculation
Overflow possible for very large quantities with high usdPrice (deltaDecimal * quantity * usdPrice before division)

core_rules: Users lock ERC20 or ETH for a configurable duration to earn schnibbles and NFTs
Lock duration is per-player, applied globally across all their tokens
During lockdrop periods, locked amounts earn NFT reveals based on token-specific nftCost
Tokens can only be unlocked after unlockTime has passed
USD price updates require 3-of-5 multisig approval
Only main accounts (not sub-accounts) can lock tokens
Tokens must be active to lock but can be inactive for unlock
economic_incentives: Longer lock durations incentivized by higher schnibble accrual rates (external to this contract)
NFT rewards proportional to locked amount during lockdrop events
Remainder from NFT cost division carries forward to next lock during lockdrop
governance_mechanisms: 5 PriceFeed roles propose/approve/disapprove USD price updates
APPROVE_THRESHOLD (default 3) approvals needed to execute price update
DISAPPROVE_THRESHOLD (default 3) disapprovals needed to cancel proposal
Thresholds configurable by admin only when no proposal is active
Proposer auto-approves and cannot approve again or disapprove

edge_cases.scenario: lockOnBehalf with 0 quantity ERC20 token to grief victim's unlock time
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should not modify existing lock parameters for the recipient or should require non-zero amount
edge_cases.actual_behavior: unlockTime is reset to block.timestamp + recipient's lockDuration, extending active locks. lastLockTime is reset. Zero-cost for ERC20 tokens (0 amount transferFrom usually succeeds).
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Attacker repeatedly calls lockOnBehalf(token, 0, victim) to indefinitely extend victim's lock, permanently freezing their funds. For ETH locks, costs only 1 wei per extension.

edge_cases.scenario: setLockDuration check uses block.timestamp instead of lastLockTime for new unlockTime comparison
edge_cases.expected_behavior: New unlockTime (lastLockTime + newDuration) should never be less than old unlockTime
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Check compares (block.timestamp + newDuration) >= old unlockTime, but sets unlockTime = lastLockTime + newDuration. After enough time passes, a small newDuration passes the check yet sets unlockTime to a past date.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: User locks for 90 days. After ~89 days, calls setLockDuration(1 day). Check: (now + 1 day ≈ lockTime + 90 days) >= unlockTime passes. New unlockTime = lockTime + 1 day = 88 days ago. User unlocks immediately, bypassing ~1 day of remaining lock. Severity scales with the ratio between old and new durations.

edge_cases.scenario: PriceFeed operator disapproves then approves the same proposal
edge_cases.expected_behavior: An operator should only be able to cast one vote (approve OR disapprove)
edge_cases.actual_behavior: disapproveUSDPrice sets disapprovals[sender] = _usdProposalId, but approveUSDPrice only checks approvals[sender]. A disapprover can subsequently approve, incrementing both counts.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: 2 colluding operators + proposer can pass any price: proposer auto-approves (1), colluder1 disapproves then approves (approval count 2), colluder2 approves (approval count 3 = threshold). The disapproval was meaningless. Conversely, reaching disapproval threshold becomes harder as disapprovers can switch to approve.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: lockedToken.quantity should reflect actual received amount
edge_cases.actual_behavior: lockedToken.quantity += _quantity (the requested amount, not the received amount). For fee-on-transfer tokens, the contract receives less but records the full amount.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Over time, total recorded quantities exceed actual contract balance. Last users to unlock cannot withdraw their full amounts. Also inflates NFT calculation during lockdrops.

edge_cases.scenario: Locking outside lockdrop period zeroes stored remainder
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Remainder from previous lockdrop should be preserved or used in next lockdrop
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Variable 'remainder' defaults to 0, and lockedToken.remainder is unconditionally set to this value. Any lock outside lockdrop sets remainder to 0.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: User loses accumulated remainder tokens toward their next NFT. Small but real value loss.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should be validated or handled gracefully
edge_cases.actual_behavior: getLockedWeightedValue computes 10^(18 - decimals) which underflows and reverts, breaking weighted value calculation for ALL users
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Admin misconfiguration (or malicious admin) DOSes the getLockedWeightedValue function, blocking reward distribution for all players

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Proposer's auto-approval (count=1) should immediately execute the price update
edge_cases.actual_behavior: _execUSDPriceUpdate is only called inside approveUSDPrice, never in proposeUSDPrice. Proposal sits with 1 approval and requires a second operator to call approveUSDPrice.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Functional bug - threshold semantics don't match implementation. With threshold 1, minimum 2 approvals are still required.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: User should be able to withdraw at least what they locked
edge_cases.actual_behavior: If token rebases down, contract balance decreases but lockedToken.quantity is unchanged. Later unlocks may fail due to insufficient balance.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: For positive rebases, excess tokens are stuck in contract. For negative rebases, last users cannot withdraw.

edge_cases.scenario: Migration manager bypass of NFT minting during lockdrop
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Migration locks should not mint NFTs (by design)
edge_cases.actual_behavior: When msg.sender == migrationManager, NFT calculation is skipped but remainder is also not calculated, so remainder stays 0
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Migrated users lose any remainder benefit. Minor value loss.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Lock on behalf should not modify recipient's personal settings
edge_cases.actual_behavior: If recipient's lockDuration is 0, _lock sets it to lockdrop.minLockDuration and saves to playerSettings, overwriting the recipient's implicit preference
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Attacker can force-set a victim's lock duration by being the first to lock on their behalf before the victim sets their own preference

assumption_violations: Assumes all ERC20 tokens transfer exactly _quantity (violated by fee-on-transfer and rebasing tokens)
Assumes configuredToken.decimals <= 18 (no validation, overflow if violated)
Assumes lockOnBehalf callers are benevolent (no recipient consent required)
Assumes block.timestamp fits in uint32 (overflows in 2106)
Assumes configuredTokenContracts array stays small enough for iteration in setLockDuration
Assumes ERC20.transfer/transferFrom always revert on failure (not true for non-standard tokens like USDT on some chains)
Assumes that checking (block.timestamp + duration >= unlockTime) is equivalent to checking (lastLockTime + duration >= unlockTime) - INCORRECT
game_theory_risks: Lock-on-behalf griefing: rational adversary can freeze victim funds indefinitely at near-zero cost
Price feed collusion: 3 of 5 operators can set arbitrary prices, manipulating weighted values and reward distribution
Disapprove-then-approve voting: reduces effective security of multisig from 3-of-5 to 2-of-5 (proposer + 1 colluder who disapproves then approves + 1 honest approver)
Lock duration manipulation allows slight early unlock, reducing effective staking security for protocol
integration_risks: accountManager.forceHarvest() is called before state updates in _lock - if it calls back into LockManager, state could be inconsistent (mitigated by nonReentrant)
nftOverlord.addReveal() called before token transfer - if it reverts, lock fails entirely (denial of service vector)
configStorage dependency - if configStorage returns unexpected values, contract behavior is undefined
migrationManager address comparison (msg.sender != address(migrationManager)) - if migrationManager is not set, any lock during lockdrop would mint NFTs
calculation_errors: setLockDuration: unlockTime = lastLockTime + _duration but check uses block.timestamp + _duration >= old unlockTime. These are not equivalent when block.timestamp >> lastLockTime.
getLockedWeightedValue: potential overflow in (deltaDecimal * quantity * usdPrice) before the /1e18 division for tokens with large supplies and high prices
NFT count uses uint16 max check (65535) but nftCost denominated in raw token units may lead to unexpected NFT counts depending on token decimals and nftCost configuration
approvalsCount and disapprovalsCount can exceed the number of unique operators due to the disapprove-then-approve loophole

token_semantics.notes: Locked via msg.value. Unlocked via payable.transfer() with 2300 gas stipend. No fee-on-transfer concern.

Contract uses raw transfer/transferFrom without SafeERC20. Does not measure actual received amount via balance delta. Tokens with fee-on-transfer, rebasing, or non-standard return values will cause accounting errors. Decimals stored in configuredTokens but not validated <= 18.

invariants.name: lock_quantity_conservation
invariants.notes: VIOLATED for fee-on-transfer tokens: sum of all lockedTokens quantities can exceed actual contract balance

invariants.name: unlock_time_monotonicity
invariants.notes: VIOLATED: setLockDuration can reduce unlockTime to before lastLockTime + original duration due to flawed check; lockOnBehalf can both extend and (in theory with short recipient duration) reduce unlockTime

invariants.name: vote_exclusivity
invariants.notes: VIOLATED: An operator can disapprove then approve the same proposal, incrementing both counters

invariants.name: remainder_preservation
invariants.notes: VIOLATED: Locking outside lockdrop period zeros remainder unconditionally

invariants.name: recipient_settings_sovereignty
invariants.notes: VIOLATED: lockOnBehalf can overwrite recipient's lockDuration if it was previously 0
